tokens.__doc: mode: npm-json | repo-file (read a JSON file from a package/repo dir) | url (fetch+merge per-theme + non-color token JSON) | manual (knowledge/tokens.json authored by hand). Canonical token shape: { "<name>": { name, cssVar, type, description, light, dark } } for themed, or { ..."value" } for shared.

components.__doc: mode: npm-json | repo-file (read generated/components.json) | url (fetch it) | manual. Accepts either { components: { <id>: {...} } } or the inner map. Each entry: { name, props:[{name,type,required,defaultValue,description}], subcomponents, importPath }.

usage.__doc: mode: package-md | repo-md (copy guidelines/*.md) | storybook-url (index.json → usage/stories.json) | manual (model authors knowledge/usage/*.md from Confluence/Docs/screenshots) | none.

icons.__doc: OPTIONAL. mode: repo-svgs (readdir *.svg → names) | json (array/object of names from path or url) | manual (author knowledge/icons.json) | none. componentPattern documents the API shape, e.g. "<Icon name=\"...\" />" or "named-export".
icons.usage: Use <Icon name="..."/> from @acme/icons; never inline <svg> or a raw icon font.

runtime.__doc: How the DS bootstraps so tokens/component styles resolve at runtime. The #1 failure mode is skipping this. classPrefix (optional) is the component CSS class namespace, e.g. "Acme-".
runtime.summary: @acme/ui ships tokens + component styles as one stylesheet. Every var(--token) and Acme-* class is undefined until '@acme/ui/styles.css' is imported once at the app root. Theming is the data-theme attribute on an ancestor. Without the import the UI renders fully unstyled.

themeAttribute.name: data-theme
themeAttribute.appliedTo: the documentElement (or any ancestor of the UI)
